compiled_state.title: harvest-worktree.sh: branch with new commits + .test-index conflict resolution -> reports 'already merged' false-negative
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Symptom
harvest-worktree.sh reported 'Branch worktree-agent-a6f2da18 is already merged — nothing to do' (and same for worktree-agent-ac99a02f) when in fact the branch's feature commit was NOT in the session branch. Verified via:

  git merge-base --is-ancestor 25ab3f28 worktree-20260418-215740 → exit 1 (NOT merged)

But harvest skipped the merge step.

## Discovered During
Sprint epic ca48-c3c3 batch 3, harvesting tasks 5b12-e68d and 4d18-2454. Both worktrees had .test-index modifications (RED-marker additions). Manual merge succeeded after detecting the issue, with .test-index auto-merging via the union driver.

## Suspected Cause
Likely the is-ancestor check in harvest-worktree.sh compares the wrong refs — possibly comparing the parent commit of the worktree branch to session HEAD instead of comparing the worktree branch tip itself. The worktree branches were created from old session HEAD (4a790d65), and that base IS an ancestor of current session HEAD — but the new commits (25ab3f28, 7408e37f) added on top of that base are not.

## Workaround
Manual merge: `git merge --no-ff worktree-agent-<id> -m "Merge ..."`. After manual merge, branches and worktrees can be cleaned up normally.

comments.body: Investigation (INTERMEDIATE, opus): Root cause: git merge-base --is-ancestor returns true when HEAD resolves to the worktree branch itself — happens when harvest is invoked with cwd inside the worktree (orchestrator's cd to session root was missed, e.g., after compact). Fix: add preflight at line 108 — resolve WORKTREE_BRANCH and HEAD to SHAs, error if equal, error if HEAD's symbolic ref == refs/heads/WORKTREE_BRANCH, error if branch ref missing. Medium confidence. DEFERRED: test-harvest-worktree.sh is flaky in test-batched.sh (blocks commit pipeline); commits on harvest-worktree.sh are currently blocked until the flakiness is resolved. User approval to either (a) fix test flakiness first, or (b) apply with a temporary RED marker.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 3 (INTERMEDIATE) — false ancestor check when harvest called with CWD inside worktree
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Hypothesis: harvest-worktree.sh is called from within the agent worktree (CWD = worktree path). When inside the worktree, git symbolic-ref HEAD returns refs/heads/WORKTREE_BRANCH. The --is-ancestor check at line 108 evaluates '--is-ancestor X X' (a commit is always an ancestor of itself) → returns 0 → falsely reports 'already merged' → exits 0 without merging. Fix: add a preflight guard checking if symbolic-ref HEAD == refs/heads/WORKTREE_BRANCH and exit 1 with a descriptive error. RED test: test_harvest_errors_when_called_from_inside_worktree (test 20) confirmed failing. Approved for implementation.
comments.author: Test
